meaning: n.抢劫,盗窃,[总称]土匪,强盗
examples: Charity is as bad as brigandage. Charity is really just as unfair to the recipient as to the donor.
As a result, there were excessive fraudulence and brigandage in the field of economy between all groups of people with various sly and brutal measures.
Short of protecting our persons and property from fraud, brigandage and like abuses through an impartial application of the law, government can only impede the natural flow of commerce.
